title: Crispy Spicy Kenyan Chicken Paella
description: A rich and flavorful dish that combines the best of Kenyan spices with the classic Spanish paella, perfect for a hangover cure.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped red bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the spicy kenyan curry powder and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the tamarind paste and tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the crushed tomatoes and chicken broth and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the chicken breast to the pan and cook until browned and cooked through, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the paella rice and cook for 5 minutes.
Transfer the pan to the oven and bake at 400°F for 20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the pan from the oven and stir in the frozen peas.
Serve the paella hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
